 {"id":"bd-29vu","title":"Create custom glamour theme matching Pi visual style","description":"Create a Pi-specific glamour theme for markdown rendering consistency.

## Current State
- glamour uses built-in Dark style
- Pi has its own color scheme in themes
- Markdown colors don't match Pi theme

## Implementation
1. Define PiGlamourStyle based on Pi theme colors
2. Map Pi theme tokens to glamour elements:
   - Headers → theme.primary
   - Links → theme.secondary
   - Code → theme.tool
   - Bold → theme.text (bold)
3. Update dynamically when Pi theme changes

## Glamour Style Elements
- Headers (h1-h6)
- Emphasis (bold, italic)
- Links
- Code blocks and inline code
- Lists
- Blockquotes
- Horizontal rules

## Integration
1. Create pi_glamour_style() function
2. Call when theme changes
3. Use in interactive.rs markdown rendering

## Test Plan
- Manual test: markdown renders with Pi theme colors
- Manual test: theme change updates markdown colors

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (custom style)
- src/theme.rs (style mapping helper)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:17.007799814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:21:23.797420864Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:21:23.797321649Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: Theme::glamour_style_config maps Pi theme colors to Glamour styles (headings/links/emphasis/code/blockquotes/lists) and interactive apply_theme refreshes markdown_style for MarkdownRenderer.  {"id":"bd-2b1f","title":"Implement pi.registerCommand() for extension slash commands","description":"Allow extensions to register custom slash commands that integrate with the TUI.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerCommand(name, {
  description: string,
  handler: (args, ctx) => Promise<void>,
  getArgumentCompletions?: (prefix) => CompletionItem[]
});
```

## Implementation Requirements
1. CommandRegistry in extension runtime to store registered commands
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerCommand' that validates and stores
3. Integration with autocomplete system (src/autocomplete.rs)
4. Integration with slash command processor (src/interactive.rs)
5. Command execution via extension runtime (JS handler)

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.registerCommand('mycommand', {...})
2. Hostcall stores in CommandRegistry
3. /mycommand shows in autocomplete suggestions
4. User types /mycommand args
5. Interactive processor detects extension command
6. Invokes JS handler with args and ExtensionCommandContext

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerCommand stores command metadata
- Integration test: registered command appears in autocomplete
- E2E test: /mycommand executes JS handler

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (CommandRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/autocomplete.rs (include extension commands)
- src/interactive.rs (dispatch to extension handler)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletCrane","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:36.671947075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:09.889724131Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:09.889661023Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b1f","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:53Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b1f","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-x4x7","title":"Task: Implement agent lifecycle event dispatch (agent_start/agent_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Agent Lifecycle Event Dispatch

## Objective

Dispatch agent_start and agent_end events at the beginning and end of agent runs.

## Background

Agent lifecycle events allow extensions to:
- Initialize resources at agent start
- Clean up resources at agent end
- Log overall agent activity
- Modify system prompts (agent_start)

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// At start of agent.run()
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_start')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_start',
        sessionId: session.id,
    });
}

// At end of agent.run() (including errors)
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        messages: conversation.messages,
        error: error?.message,
    });
}
```

## Implementation

### In Agent Run Method (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<AgentResult> {
        // Get event dispatcher if extensions are loaded
        let event_dispatcher = self.extension_manager
            .as_ref()
            .map(|em| em.event_dispatcher());
        
        // Dispatch agent_start
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_start") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                };
                dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Run agent loop
        let result = self.run_loop(&event_dispatcher).await;
        
        // Dispatch agent_end (even on error)
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("agent_end") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::AgentEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    messages: self.session.get_messages(),
                    error: result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string()),
                };
                let _ = dispatcher.dispatch::<()>(event).await;
                // Ignore dispatch errors during cleanup
            }
        }
        
        result
    }
}
```

## Event Data

### agent_start
```json
{
    "type": "agent_start",
    "sessionId": "abc-123"
}
```

### agent_end
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": null
}
```

### agent_end (with error)
```json
{
    "type": "agent_end",
    "sessionId": "abc-123",
    "messages": [...],
    "error": "Rate limit exceeded"
}
```

## Handler Examples

```javascript
// Track agent sessions
ctx.on('agent_start', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_start', { sessionId: event.sessionId });
});

ctx.on('agent_end', (event) => {
    analytics.track('agent_end', {
        sessionId: event.sessionId,
        messageCount: event.messages.length,
        hadError: \!\!event.error,
    });
    
    if (event.error) {
        errorReporter.report(event.error);
    }
});
```

## Important Considerations

### agent_end Must Always Fire

Even if the agent loop errors or panics, agent_end should fire:

```rust
// Use a guard or finally pattern
struct AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    dispatcher: Option<&'a EventDispatcher>,
    session_id: String,
    fired: bool,
}

impl<'a> Drop for AgentEndGuard<'a> {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if \!self.fired {
            if let Some(dispatcher) = self.dispatcher {
                // Fire agent_end synchronously in drop
                // (or use a channel to signal)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Error Handling

- agent_start errors: Should abort the agent run
- agent_end errors: Should be logged but not propagate

## Testing

1. Unit test: agent_start fires before loop
2. Unit test: agent_end fires after successful run
3. Unit test: agent_end fires after error
4. Unit test: messages included in agent_end
5. Unit test: error included when agent fails
6. Integration test: Full lifecycle

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch feature)

- [ ] agent_end dispatched at run end
- [ ] agent_end fires even on error

-## Beads Workflow Integration
-
-This project uses [beads_viewer](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer) for issue tracking. Issues are stored in `.beads/` and tracked in git.
-
-### Essential Commands
-
-```bash
-# View issues (launches TUI - avoid in automated sessions)
-bv
-
-# CLI commands for agents (use these instead)
-bd ready              # Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
-bd list --status=open # All open issues
-bd show <id>          # Full issue details with dependencies
-bd create --title="..." --type=task --priority=2
-bd update <id> --status=in_progress
-bd close <id> --reason="Completed"
-bd close <id1> <id2>  # Close multiple issues at once
-bd sync               # Commit and push changes
-```
-
-### Workflow Pattern
-
-1. **Start**: Run `bd ready` to find actionable work
-2. **Claim**: Use `bd update <id> --status=in_progress`
-3. **Work**: Implement the task
-4. **Complete**: Use `bd close <id>`
-5. **Sync**: Always run `bd sync` at session end
-
-### Key Concepts
-
-- **Dependencies**: Issues can block other issues. `bd ready` shows only unblocked work.
-- **Priority**: P0=critical, P1=high, P2=medium, P3=low, P4=backlog (use numbers, not words)
-- **Types**: task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs
-- **Blocking**: `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` to add dependencies
-
-### Session Protocol
-
-**Before ending any session, run this checklist:**
-
-```bash
-git status              # Check what changed
-git add <files>         # Stage code changes
-bd sync                 # Commit beads changes
-git commit -m "..."     # Commit code
-bd sync                 # Commit any new beads changes
-git push                # Push to remote
-```
-
-### Best Practices
-
-- Check `bd ready` at session start to find available work
-- Update status as you work (in_progress → closed)
-- Create new issues with `bd create` when you discover tasks
-- Use descriptive titles and set appropriate priority/type
-- Always `bd sync` before ending session
